title: ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) Calculator
metaDescription: Calculate your Return on Ad Spend ratio and percentage instantly in your browser.
shortDescription: Calculate ROAS ratio, percentage, and net profit from ad campaign spend and revenue.

guideSections.heading: Why a 4:1 ROAS Isn't Automatically Profitable
guideSections.body: ROAS measures revenue generated per dollar of ad spend, but it doesn't account for cost of goods sold, fulfillment costs, or payment processing fees. A 4:1 ROAS on a product with thin margins can still result in a net loss once those costs are factored in separately.

guideSections.heading: Why Comparing ROAS Across Channels Can Mislead
guideSections.body: Different channels often serve different parts of the funnel — a retargeting campaign with a high ROAS is reaching warm traffic that may have converted anyway, while a prospecting campaign with a lower ROAS is generating net-new demand. Comparing the two on ROAS alone ignores what each channel is actually contributing.
